decisions.relationship_evidence: Proposes a new network-based compartmental model of spatial RVF transmission and evaluates it against the 2010 South African outbreak.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicitly develops and analyzes a new spatial transmission-model formulation.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Employs an SEIR metapopulation model to simulate H1N1 spread through an airline network and compare flight-cancellation strategies.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application to influenza spread and travel interventions.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Presents an epidemic model for directed metapopulation networks and analyzes directionality and modularity effects.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit methodological development for epidemics on directed networks.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses stochastic reaction-diffusion simulations and mean-field analysis on heterogeneous metapopulation networks to assess a control strategy.
decisions.lineage_evidence: The proposed contribution is a control-allocation strategy rather than a source for the metapopulation-model concept itself.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Tests a spatial network reproduction-matrix approach against computations for metapopulation networks and cholera outbreaks.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Provides explicit analytical methodology for spatially connected transmission systems.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Proposes and validates a high-resolution metapopulation model coupling mosquito dynamics, transmission, and human and mosquito mobility.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicitly develops a metapopulation transmission model validated with Réunion chikungunya data.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Develops a theoretical framework coupling safety-information-driven mobility with metapopulation epidemic dynamics.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit methodological framework development.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Presents a flexible metapopulation disease-spread model incorporating livestock-density variation and high-mixing events.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicitly develops a flexible transmission-model formulation.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Extends multitype-network methods to combine contact-network and metapopulation structure and derives epidemic-front properties and thresholds.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit theoretical extension of metapopulation epidemic modeling.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses a metapopulation modelling framework informed by British poultry contact data to compare compartmentalization and zoning controls for avian influenza.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application to highly pathogenic avian influenza control.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses spatially structured poultry models to show how flock metapopulations can sustain influenza A/H5N1.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application to influenza persistence in poultry.
supported_label_variants: theoretical models of spatial population structure

decisions.relationship_evidence: Presents a multi-host stochastic epidemic metapopulation model and derives a global-invasion threshold under heterogeneous mixing and travel.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Provides a complete mathematical formulation and general theoretical framework.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Presents a metapopulation model for sylvatic Trypanosoma cruzi transmission across regions and overlapping host-vector cycles.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicitly develops and analyzes a multi-region transmission model.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Builds a multispecies metapopulation transmission model to investigate EBLV-1 persistence and species-specific roles.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit development of a disease-specific multispecies model.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses metapopulation comparisons within Israel to corroborate a fitted influenza forecasting model.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application to multiannual influenza forecasting.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Presents a framework for evaluating random and targeted interventions across spatial patches in metapopulation models.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit methodological framework for intervention analysis in metapopulation models.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Develops a mean-field framework for epidemic spread across interconnected metapopulation networks and analyzes mobility controls.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit theoretical framework development.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Introduces deterministic and stochastic seasonal SIR metapopulation models with vectors, dispersal, and an Allee effect.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicitly introduces and analyzes new model formulations.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Designs and parameterizes a spatial deterministic metapopulation model coupled to a cattle-trade network for RVF transmission.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit disease-specific model development with observational calibration.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Develops a discrete-time stochastic n-patch epidemic model allowing arbitrary infectious-period distributions.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit methodological model development and analytical comparison.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Develops NetEpi to infer transmission networks between subpopulations, but does not explicitly use, evaluate, or develop a metapopulation transmission model.
decisions.lineage_evidence: The record concerns network inference at a metapopulation level, a neighboring task rather than lineage for the model concept.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Analyzes SIS dynamics on metapopulation networks and derives and simulates sufficient conditions for the endemic threshold.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit theoretical analysis of metapopulation epidemic dynamics.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Reports a factorial laboratory experiment on parasite-infected ciliate metapopulations rather than using or developing a transmission model.
decisions.lineage_evidence: An experimental metapopulation study is not a lineage source for the modeling concept.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Integrates empirically estimated contact scaling into a reaction-diffusion metapopulation framework and derives a global-invasion threshold.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit analytical development of a metapopulation transmission framework.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Describes a new next-generation-matrix approach for intervention design in a general metapopulation SIRS model.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit methodological development for metapopulation transmission models.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses artificial and empirical metapopulation networks to develop and test inference of pandemic invasion pathways.
decisions.lineage_evidence: The contribution is an invasion-pathway inference algorithm rather than a source for metapopulation transmission models.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Develops a stochastic event-driven metapopulation model coupled to a swine-influenza epidemiological model for within-herd persistence.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit disease-specific model development.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Generalizes mixing functions in metapopulation models and applies a multilevel SEIR formulation to influenza vaccination.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit methodological generalization of metapopulation mixing schemes.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Develops a spatially explicit metapopulation model to analyze joint effects of host and pathogen dispersal on disease establishment and invasion.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit spatial transmission-model development.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Incorporates information propagation and mobility patterns into a metapopulation epidemic model and analyzes resulting thresholds.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit extension of the metapopulation model framework.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses a data-driven global stochastic epidemic model, but the title and abstract do not explicitly identify it as a metapopulation transmission model.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concept assignment alone cannot establish metapopulation-model lineage.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Develops a stochastic metapopulation model of two swine-influenza viruses within farrow-to-finish herds.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit disease-specific model development and control assessment.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses a landscape-scale modeling framework to analyze host-pathogen dynamics, extinction, and recolonization across a boreal-toad metapopulation.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application to chytridiomycosis in boreal toads.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Analyzes constrained optimization of reproduction numbers in metapopulation models with general heterogeneous mixing functions.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit methodological analysis of optimization feasibility in metapopulation models.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Develops and validates an ensemble spatial forecast system combining human-mobility data with a metapopulation influenza model.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit forecast-system development and validation using a metapopulation model.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Extends a stochastic SIS epidemic model to spatial networks, yielding a stochastic epidemic metapopulation model and associated controls.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit methodological extension and comparison with deterministic models.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Presents a random-walker metapopulation epidemic model and derives analytical and numerical solutions for several graph types.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit model formulation and theoretical analysis.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Derives a final-size relation for a metapopulation model with general mixing and analyzes effects of heterogeneity.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit analytical development for metapopulation transmission models.
named_source_mentions: J.W. Glasser, Z. Feng, S.B. Omer, P.J. Smith, and L.E. Rodewald
The effect of heterogeneity in uptake of the measles, mumps, and rubella vaccine on the potential for outbreaks of measles: A modelling study

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses two metapopulation models to investigate MRSA outbreaks and community-correctional-facility feedback.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application to MRSA transmission in correctional facilities.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses a commuting-linked metapopulation model to study provincial spread and interventions for 2009 H1N1 in Korea.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application to national influenza spread.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Fits a metapopulation model of PPRV spread to a nationwide Ethiopian serological survey and evaluates elimination coverage.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application to peste des petits ruminants transmission and vaccination.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Studies epidemic spreading with migration and demographics in a reaction-diffusion metapopulation framework using theory and simulation.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit theoretical treatment of metapopulation epidemic dynamics.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Develops and calibrates a deterministic metapopulation model with latent-dog importation for rabies in N'Djamena.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit disease-specific model development and calibration.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Proposes a stochastic diffusion framework that models disease spread across metapopulations using human-mobility pathways.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit methodological framework development and simulation evaluation.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Simulates mosquito control under mosquito metapopulation dynamics, but does not explicitly describe the disease-transmission model as a metapopulation model.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Mosquito population metapopulation dynamics alone do not establish lineage for the exact transmission-model concept.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Develops a generalized metapopulation framework for quantifying pathogen transfer and circulation between hosts and environments.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit customizable framework development.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses an age- and province-stratified mechanistic metapopulation model to evaluate measles immunization strategies in China.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application to measles elimination policy.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The title explicitly identifies the work as a meta-population modelling study evaluating measles vaccination policies in China.
decisions.lineage_evidence: The title supports a concrete measles-control application, although no abstract is available.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses a stochastic individual-based metapopulation model to analyze mobility effects on helminth elimination and reintroduction.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application to soil-transmitted helminth control.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Theoretically analyzes intervention thresholds in an SIR metapopulation model and validates results by network simulations.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit theoretical analysis of intervention thresholds in metapopulation models.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Establishes and analyzes an epidemic model on metapopulation networks with heterogeneous diffusion rates.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit mathematical model development.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses a calibrated global metapopulation disease-transmission model to project COVID-19 travel-restriction effects.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application to early COVID-19 spread and travel controls.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses a networked dynamic metapopulation model with mobility data and Bayesian inference to estimate undocumented SARS-CoV-2 infections.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application to SARS-CoV-2 transmission inference.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Reviews progress, evidence, and applications in networked metapopulation epidemiology but does not itself perform an allowed originating, applying, critiquing, correcting, diagnostic, or implementation role.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit overview of spatial networked-metapopulation epidemiology.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Proposes a deterministic general metapopulation-network model and two moment-closure assumptions for infectious-disease transmission.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit general-model and closure-method development.
supported_label_variants: infectious diseases model on heterogeneous metapopulation network

decisions.relationship_evidence: Proposes an SIS transmission model on a metapopulation network coupled to its second-neighbor network.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit transmission-model extension addressing indirect travel.
supported_label_variants: infectious diseases models on metapopulation networks

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses a mean-field approach to investigate time-varying mobility effects on epidemic dynamics in heterogeneous metapopulation networks.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit theoretical analysis of mobility in metapopulation epidemic dynamics.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Studies epidemic spreading in metapopulation networks with heterogeneous local infection rates using mean-field analysis and simulation.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit theoretical analysis of a metapopulation model variant.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses a spatial metacommunity SEIR-like model, a neighboring formulation that the text does not identify as a metapopulation transmission model.
decisions.lineage_evidence: The inspected text does not establish exact-concept lineage.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Develops a networked meta-population model using mobile-phone data to simulate timing, strength, and duration of COVID-19 controls.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit data-driven model development.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses a spatially hierarchical meta-population COVID-19 model to assess how crowding shapes epidemic trajectories.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application to COVID-19 spatial heterogeneity.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Develops a meta-population network model-inference system to estimate SARS-CoV-2 infection rates and fatality risk in New York City.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit model-inference system development.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses a metapopulation framework to explain infection incidence and antibiotic resistance across 357 connected hospital wards.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application to hospital infection and antimicrobial-resistance dynamics.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Introduces a metapopulation SEIR model integrating fine-grained dynamic mobility networks for COVID-19 simulation.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit development of a mobility-network metapopulation model.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Develops a stochastic meta-population model representing within-feedlot contact structure and FMD transmission routes.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit disease-specific transmission-model development.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses metapopulation analysis and an infectious model to simulate rabies spread among observed dog groups.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Proposes a metapopulation network model for evaluating network-structure-based epidemic interventions.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit model and intervention-strategy development.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses age- and region-stratified metapopulation modeling to evaluate rubella immunization strategies and indirect effects.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application to rubella and congenital-rubella-syndrome control in China.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Develops a spatially explicit metapopulation model to predict host-pathogen disease dynamics from extensive ecological data.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Presents an all-Ireland metapopulation network model coupling travel links with local SIRX dynamics for SARS-CoV-2.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit network-model development and case-study demonstration.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Develops a metapopulation model coupling social-group demography and pathogen transmission, parameterized for Yellowstone wolves.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit general model development with disease applications.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Introduces analytical models combining connectivity, reproduction number, and mobility to quantify superspreader capacity in metapopulation networks.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit methodological model development and simulation validation.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Develops and fits a stochastic spatial metapopulation model for sheep-scab transmission using farm and movement data.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit adaptable transmission-model development and validation.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Compares alternative human-movement formulations within infectious-disease metapopulation models and shows that model choice can materially alter outcomes.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit evaluation of how movement-model choice affects metapopulation-model predictions.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses an SIR model to explore pathogen transmission and host evolution in a spatially connected host metapopulation.
decisions.lineage_evidence: A generic theoretical application rather than a concrete infectious application or foundational source.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Develops a mobility-augmented stochastic SEIR-style framework addressing intracounty spatial heterogeneity in metapopulation epidemic modeling.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit methodological framework development.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Develops and calibrates a stochastic data-informed meta-population model for Belgian COVID-19 mixing, mobility, and exit strategies.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses a risk-structured metapopulation epidemic model to compare vaccination scenarios with mobility restrictions for COVID-19.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Develops a general method for bidirectional commuting within metapopulation epidemic models without tracking individuals separately.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Runs data-driven and synthetic epidemic simulations on geometric metapopulation networks to compare seeding scenarios.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses a metapopulation transmission-dynamic model to evaluate COVID-19 vaccination strategies in a prison.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Derives message-passing theory for metapopulation modeling and proposes a method for identifying influential spreaders.
